mergedPRs.title: fix: legacy dispatch HTML fixes and mobile improvements (#49, #50, #51, #52)
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body: ## UX/UI Audit Round 2 ‚Äî Fixes Closes #49, #50, #51, #52 ### Changes **#49 ‚Äî Fix unclosed `` tags in legacy dispatches** Legacy R2-stored dispatches (generated by the old template) have malformed HTML: headline anchor tags are never closed before the heading element closes. This adds a `fixUnclosedHeadlineLinks()` post-processing function in the serving layer that repairs this for all served dispatches without requiring regeneration. **#50 ‚Äî Override `--link: #1a1a8c` blue in legacy dispatch CSS** Legacy dispatches define `--link: #1a1a8c` which creates blue hover colors on header kicker links. The injected style block now overrides this with `body { --link: var(--ink, #0f0f0f) !important; }` to enforce the ink-only link rule. **#51 ‚Äî Reduce homepage hero top margin on mobile** The `.hero` class had `margin: 64px auto 0` with no mobile override. Added `@media (max-width: 640px) { .hero { margin-top: 28px; } }` to reduce wasted above-the-fold space on 375px screens. **#52 ‚Äî Improve share links touch targets in dispatch footer** Removed `justify-content: flex-end` from the share links row (links now flow left-to-right naturally) and added `min-height: 44px; padding: 8px 0; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;` to meet minimum touch target size on mobile. ### Testing - `wrangler deploy --dry-run` passes (build succeeds, TypeScript compiles) - Regex in `fixUnclosedHeadlineLinks` only closes `headline-link` anchors; skips if `` already present

mergedPRs.title: feat: Playfair Display logo font for readability (#30)
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Closes #30

## What

Replaces **UnifrakturMaguntia** (fraktur blackletter) with **Playfair Display 900 italic** across all pages in `src/pages.ts`.

## Why

The fraktur 'z' glyph in UnifrakturMaguntia is ambiguous ‚Äî "gitzette" cold-reads as "gitkette" or "gittette". Playfair Display keeps the newspaper-editorial personality while being unambiguously readable.

## Changes

- `IMG_FIX_STYLE` (injected into R2-stored dispatch HTML): font URL + breadcrumb `<a>` style
- `homePage`: Google Fonts link + `.masthead` CSS class
- `dispatchPage`: Google Fonts link + `.breadcrumb-logo` CSS class
- `noDispatchPage`: Google Fonts link + inline logo `<a>` style
- `weekNotFoundPage`: Google Fonts link + inline logo `<a>` style
- `errorPage` / other utility pages: same pattern

Font: `Playfair Display` weight 900, italic ‚Äî loaded via Google Fonts with `ital,wght@0,700;0,900;1,700;1,900` axis.

All clamp font-size values are **unchanged**.

mergedPRs.title: feat: breadcrumb nav gitzette / @user / week
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Closes #9

## What

Implements 3-level breadcrumb navigation in the black top bar on dispatch pages:

```
gitzette / @username / 2026-W13
```

- **gitzette** ‚Üí links to `/`, rendered in UnifrakturMaguntia ~22px
- **@username** ‚Üí links to `/${username}`, IBM Plex Mono muted
- **2026-W13** ‚Üí current page, no link, highlighted white + bold
- Separators: ` / ` in muted grey (`#555`)
- `flex-wrap: wrap` on breadcrumb div ‚Äî safe on narrow mobile screens, no overflow

## Where

Two code paths both now show proper breadcrumb:
1. **`dispatchPage()`** template (already had it, verified correct)
2. **Inline HTML dispatches** in `fetchAndServeDispatch()` ‚Äî this was the gap: visitor bar showed only `@username` plain text, owner bar showed `@username ¬∑ week_key ¬∑ generated date` with no links or logo

Also injects UnifrakturMaguntia + IBM Plex Mono Google Fonts into inline HTML dispatch `<head>` so the font renders correctly there.

mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Closes #1

Comprehensive fix for all issues found in the UI/UX audit of gitzette.online.

## What's fixed

### Mobile / Overflow
- **#1** Nav bars now have `flex-wrap:wrap;gap:12px` so week navigation wraps instead of overflowing on narrow viewports
- **#3** `.paper` card gets `overflow-x:hidden` + `@media (max-width:640px)` to remove side margins/padding on phones  
- **#4 #9 #10** Inject `img{max-width:100%!important;height:auto!important}` + table width guard into the `<head>` of every R2-served dispatch document; also added `.content img` CSS rule in the fallback template

### Typography
- **#6** Replace all bare `monospace` with `'IBM Plex Mono',monospace` in nav bars, footers, and utility pages ‚Äî consistent with the design system

### Navigation
- **#7** Week nav arrow links now have `opacity:.75` + `onmouseover/onmouseout` to show hover state
- **#8** Non-owner viewer bar in both code paths now uses `flex-wrap:wrap` and `'IBM Plex Mono'` ‚Äî consistent with owner bar

### Spacing
- **#11** Hardcoded `height:40px` spacer changed to `min-height:48px` so bar wrapping on mobile doesn't cause content overlap

### Contrast
- **#14** `creatorFooter` link color improved from `#aaa` (‚âà3.0:1) to `#ccc` (‚âà4.6:1) ‚Äî meets WCAG AA for small text

### Missing Viewport
- **#13** All 4 utility pages (`noDispatch`, `weekNotFound`, `generating`, `notFound`) now have `<meta name="viewport">` + `padding:24px;text-align:center` for proper mobile layout

## Rules followed
- Pure CSS/HTML changes only ‚Äî no new dependencies
- IBM Plex Mono + ink/paper color scheme preserved  
- No dispatch content/copy changed

openPRs.title: feat: article feedback/review system
openPRs.author: NikolayS
openPRs.body:
## What this adds

### D1 migration
- `article_feedback` table: stores per-article ratings (üëç/-1, üëé/+1), optional complaint text, source (`human`/`model`)
- Index on `(rating, source)` for fast example queries

### Review UI ‚Äî `GET /review/:username/:week_key`
- Auth-gated: dispatch owner (session cookie) or admin (`x-session-secret` header)
- Parses `<h2>`/`<h3>` headlines + first paragraph from R2 dispatch HTML
- Shows headline, first 2 sentences of body, thumbs up / thumbs down buttons
- Thumbs up: solid dark on click. Thumbs down: red border on click
- Optional complaint text input per article
- Approve & Publish button (owner/admin only)
- Pre-fills existing ratings on reload
- Newspaper aesthetic: IBM Plex Mono + Playfair Display, `--ink`/`--paper` colors

### API endpoints
- `POST /review/:username/:week_key` ‚Äî save article rating + complaint
- `GET /review/:username/:week_key/data` ‚Äî JSON of all feedback for a dispatch (auth required)
- `GET /review/examples` ‚Äî public JSON of top 5 gold + top 3 bad articles (no auth)

### Deployed
Worker already deployed to Cloudflare. D1 migration applied to remote DB.

name: rpg
description: rpg ‚Äî modern Postgres terminal written in Rust. psql-compatible, with built-in DBA diagnostics and AI assistant

releases.name: v0.8.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.8.0

### Enhanced EXPLAIN Visualization

- **Summary header** ‚Äî execution time, planning time, rows, buffer stats at a glance
- **Automatic issue detection** ‚Äî warnings for seq scans on large tables, row estimate errors >10x, sorts spilling to disk, hash batches >1, high filter removal, parallel workers not launched
- **Colorized plan** ‚Äî full PostgreSQL EXPLAIN output with ANSI color coding by time percentage and ‚öÝ markers on problem nodes
- **Hot path highlighting** ‚Äî most expensive node chain bolded
- **EXPLAIN without ANALYZE** ‚Äî shows estimated costs when actual timing unavailable
- **Limit-aware** ‚Äî no false positive warnings on Limit node children
- **`\pset explain_format raw|enhanced`** ‚Äî toggle between psql-compatible and enhanced views

### EXPLAIN Plan Sharing

- **`\explain share depesz`** ‚Äî post plan to explain.depesz.com in one command
- **`\explain share dalibo`** ‚Äî post plan to explain.dalibo.com
- **`\explain share pgmustard`** ‚Äî post plan to pgMustard (requires PGMUSTARD_API_KEY)

### Markdown Output

- **`\pset format markdown`** / **`--markdown`** ‚Äî GitHub-flavored markdown tables

### Lua Custom Commands

- **User-defined `\` commands** via Lua scripts (build with `--features lua`)
- API: `rpg.query()`, `rpg.print()`, `rpg.pager()`, `rpg.version()`, `rpg.dbname()`
- Example commands: `\slow_mean`, `\slow_total`, `\table_info`
- **`\commands`** ‚Äî list all custom commands

### Better History

- **`\s`** ‚Äî numbered, syntax-highlighted history through the pager
- **`\s pattern`** ‚Äî filter history by pattern
- **`\s filename`** ‚Äî save history to file

### Tab Completion

- All rpg-specific backslash commands now complete with Tab

### Fixes

- TxState stuck after /ask (#683)
- Pager ghost characters (#682)
- Multiline WHERE safety detection (#680)
- Double backslash in \? help (#688)
- Config messages suppressed in scripting mode (#694)
- \explain share URL extraction (#670)
- Docker build (#677)
- Legacy "samo" references removed (#678)
- macOS release builds (#668)
- Codecov integration (#660, #679, #687)

### External Pager

- **pspg integration** ‚Äî `\set PAGER 'pspg --style=22'` for horizontal scrolling, column cursor, 20+ themes

### PostgreSQL Compatibility

Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

Install: `cargo install --git https://github.com/NikolayS/rpg.git`

releases.name: v0.7.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.7.0

### Security & Safety

- **All `/ask` queries wrapped in read-only transaction** ‚Äî every query executed by `/ask` is wrapped in `start transaction read only`, providing database-level protection against accidental mutations (#626, #634)
- **DDL/DML blocked in `/ask` mode** ‚Äî write queries are refused in all `/ask` execution paths including text2sql mode (#631)
- **`\t2s` write queries restored** ‚Äî text2sql mode correctly allows write queries with explicit confirmation (`Execute write query? [y/N/e]`), fixing the regression from the `/ask` safety changes (#646)
- **Auto-rollback stale transactions** ‚Äî internal transactions from `/ask` are automatically rolled back on error or cancellation (#639)
- **Ctrl-C cancels LLM requests** ‚Äî pressing Ctrl-C during AI API calls now aborts the request immediately (#641)
- **Configurable LLM timeout** ‚Äî `ai.timeout` config option (default 30s) prevents hanging on unresponsive AI providers (#640)
- **SSH tunnel connection timeout** ‚Äî `ssh_tunnel.connect_timeout` config option (default 15s) (#640)

### Features

- **Built-in pager for backslash commands** ‚Äî `\?`, `\h`, `\d` family, `\dba` now route through the scrollable TUI pager (#622, #629)
- **Pager macOS fix** ‚Äî resolved kqueue initialization error on macOS (#635)

### Fixes

- **Zero-column SELECT results now displayed** ‚Äî `select from t where ...` correctly shows `--\n(N rows)` matching psql (#644)
- **`is_write_query` handles trailing semicolons** ‚Äî `VACUUM;`, `DELETE;` etc. now correctly classified as write queries (#645)
- **`\dba redundant-idx`** ‚Äî corrected column name (#620, #621)
- **`\dba locks`** ‚Äî fixed `waitstart` column reference (#620)
- **`\dba progress vacuum` PG 17 compat** ‚Äî handle renamed columns (#620, #621)
- **Cancelled destructive statements no longer trigger schema refresh** (#619)
- **`/fix` includes error context in text2sql mode** (#617)
- **Pager exit restores cursor and scroll region** (#616)
- **Yolo mode suppresses AI explanation text** (#614)
- **`\dba` menu Ctrl-C handling** (#608)
- **Exec mode reset when switching from yolo** (#607)

### Testing

- **59+ new tests** ‚Äî comprehensive unit and integration tests (#628, #644, #645, #646)

### Command prefix convention

- `\` commands run locally against the database (`\d`, `\dba`, `\?`, etc.)
- `/` commands call an external AI API (`/ask`, `/fix`, `/explain`, `/optimize`)

### PostgreSQL compatibility

Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

releases.name: v0.6.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.6.0

### Security & Safety

- **`/ask` queries wrapped in read-only transaction** ‚Äî every query executed by `/ask` is now wrapped in `start transaction read only`, providing database-level protection against accidental mutations even if query classification fails (#623, #626, #634)
- **DDL/DML blocked in `/ask` mode** ‚Äî write queries (CREATE, DROP, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, etc.) are refused in all `/ask` execution paths including text2sql mode (#631)
- **Auto-rollback stale transactions** ‚Äî if an `/ask` query errors or is cancelled, the internal transaction is automatically rolled back so the session never stays in `idle-in-tx` state (#639)

### Features

- **Built-in pager for backslash commands** ‚Äî `\?`, `\h`, `\d` family, `\dba`, and other commands now route output through the scrollable TUI pager when content exceeds terminal height (#622, #629)
- **Configurable LLM timeout** ‚Äî new `ai.timeout` config option (default 30s) prevents rpg from hanging on unresponsive AI providers (#640)
- **SSH tunnel connection timeout** ‚Äî new `ssh_tunnel.connect_timeout` config option (default 15s) (#640)
- **Ctrl-C cancels LLM requests** ‚Äî pressing Ctrl-C during an AI API call now aborts the request and returns to the prompt immediately (#641)

### Fixes

- **Pager macOS fix** ‚Äî resolved "Failed to initialize input reader" error caused by macOS kqueue refusing to register `/dev/tty` file descriptors (#635)
- **`\dba redundant-idx` fix** ‚Äî corrected column name (`s.relname` instead of `s.tablename`) (#620, #621)
- **`\dba locks` fix** ‚Äî fixed `waitstart` column reference (belongs to `pg_locks`, not `pg_stat_activity`) (#620)
- **`\dba progress vacuum` PG 17 compat** ‚Äî handle renamed columns `max_dead_item_ids`/`num_dead_item_ids` (#620, #621)
- **Cancelled destructive statements no longer trigger schema refresh** (#619)
- **`/fix` includes error context in text2sql mode** (#617)
- **Pager exit restores cursor and scroll region** (#616)
- **Yolo mode suppresses AI explanation text** (#614)
- **`\dba` menu Ctrl-C handling and SQL quoting** (#608)
- **Exec mode reset when switching from yolo** (#607)

### Testing

- **51 new tests** ‚Äî comprehensive unit and integration tests for `is_write_query` classification and CLI flags (#628)

### Command prefix convention

- `\` commands run locally against the database (`\d`, `\dba`, `\?`, etc.)
- `/` commands call an external AI API (`/ask`, `/fix`, `/explain`, `/optimize`)

### PostgreSQL compatibility

Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

releases.name: v0.5.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.5.0

### Features
- **Multi-category SQL syntax highlighting** ‚Äî keywords, strings, operators in distinct colors
- **Colorized error/warning/notice prefixes** ‚Äî ERROR in red, WARNING in yellow, NOTICE in cyan
- **Auto-detect AI provider** ‚Äî automatically picks up ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or OLLAMA_API_KEY from environment
- **Full-width status bar** ‚Äî status line extends to terminal width

### Fixes
- **Ctrl+C cancels `/fix` Execute prompt** ‚Äî no longer executes query on interrupt
- **`\version` shows server version** ‚Äî displays both rpg and PostgreSQL server versions
- **`\copyright` shows rpg copyright + PostgreSQL link**

### Housekeeping
- Removed autonomous agent references (AAA, O/S/A) ‚Äî rpg is a focused terminal tool with light AI integration
- README improvements: badges, real `/fix` and `/optimize` examples, accurate feature list
- License: updated copyright to Nikolay Samokhvalov <nik@samokhvalov.com>

### PostgreSQL compatibility
Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

mergedPRs.title: feat(repl): implement \s command for history display and save
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Adds psql-compatible `\s` meta-command with enhanced capabilities beyond plain psql
- `\s` ‚Äî displays numbered, syntax-highlighted history through the pager
- `\s filename` ‚Äî saves raw history to a file (heuristic: path contains `/`, `.`, `~`, or has a file extension)
- `\s pattern` ‚Äî grep-like case-insensitive filter with match summary (e.g. `\s orders` shows only queries containing "orders")

## What makes this better than psql's `\s`

- **Numbered entries** ‚Äî each line shows its history number for easy reference
- **Syntax highlighting** ‚Äî SQL keywords are highlighted when written to screen
- **Pattern filtering** ‚Äî `\s pattern` filters history inline without needing external grep
- **Match summary** ‚Äî filter results show `-- N of M entries match "pattern"`
- **Pager integration** ‚Äî long history routes through the existing `maybe_page` pager

## Implementation notes

- Reads from the history file (`~/.rpg_history` or `$PSQL_HISTORY`) rather than from the in-memory `rustyline::Editor`, so it works from any dispatch path without needing to pass the editor reference through the call chain
- New `MetaCmd::History(Option<String>)` variant in `metacmd.rs`
- Parser added to `parse_s_family` ‚Äî handled before `\sf`/`\sv` to avoid prefix conflicts, but after the longer prefixes (`\sql`, `\session`, `\set`)
- Handler `dispatch_history` and helper `read_history_entries` / `arg_is_filepath` added in `repl/mod.rs`
- `\s` already present in `BACKSLASH_CMDS` tab-completion array in `complete.rs`
- Help text updated in `\?`

mergedPRs.title: fix(ci): add codecov.yml and workflow_dispatch to fix badge unknown
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Root cause analysis

The Codecov badge started showing \"unknown\" after PR #679 (which correctly fixed the token issue). The diagnosis reveals **two compounding problems**:

### Problem 1: Codecov backend processing failure (transient)

All three recent commits on main (`e066f1`, `b9b339`, `bf55513`) have uploads that **succeeded** (HTTP 200, `Process Upload complete`) but Codecov's worker never ingested them into its database. Evidence:

- Codecov API returns `"state": null, "totals": null` for these commits
- The last successfully processed commit is `c06779` at 18:58 UTC
- All uploads after 18:58 UTC sit in GCS `shelter/github/NikolayS:::rpg/` but are unprocessed
- Codecov API is returning 503 at time of investigation, confirming a backend issue

The uploads themselves are working correctly ‚Äî this is a Codecov-side processing outage.

### Problem 2: No way to manually re-trigger CI

Without `workflow_dispatch`, there is no way to re-run the Coverage job after Codecov recovers, without making an empty code push.

## Changes

- **`codecov.yml`** ‚Äî new config file:
  - `carryforward: true` on the `unit` flag: Codecov reuses coverage from the last successful commit when a new report hasn't been processed yet (prevents badge going to "unknown" during delays)
  - `if_not_found: success` on project/patch status: prevents CI status from failing when Codecov hasn't processed the commit yet
  - `ignore` list for non-code directories
- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** ‚Äî add `workflow_dispatch` trigger so the full CI (including Coverage) can be manually re-run once Codecov's processing queue recovers

## What to do after merge

Once this PR is merged, go to the Actions tab and manually trigger the CI workflow (`workflow_dispatch`) to re-upload coverage for the new HEAD commit. This should populate Codecov and fix the badge.

**If Codecov is still down**, the badge will remain "unknown" until Codecov recovers. After recovery, Codecov may retroactively process the queued uploads from GCS, or a new manual trigger will be needed.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes on this PR
- [ ] After merge, manually trigger CI via Actions tab ‚Üí the Coverage job uploads coverage
- [ ] Codecov badge changes from "unknown" to a percentage

Copyright 2026

mergedPRs.title: fix(explain): fix \explain share URL extraction for depesz and dalibo
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Fixes #667.

## Root causes

Two bugs caused both `\explain share` commands to fail silently.

### Bug 1 ‚Äî dalibo: reqwest default redirect policy swallows the 302

`upload_dalibo` used `reqwest::Client::new()` which follows 302 redirects
automatically. On redirect, reqwest re-issued the request as POST to the
plan page URL, which returned 400 Bad Request (or in some cases, followed
through to a 200 HTML page). The `status.is_redirection()` check never
fired, JSON extraction failed, and the error was returned.

**Fix:** use `redirect::Policy::none()` in `upload_dalibo`, identical to
what `upload_depesz` already does correctly.

### Bug 2 ‚Äî depesz: plan indentation stripped, depesz rejects the plan

`execute_query_interactive` called `strip_psql_table_format()` on the
captured psql output and stored the result in `last_explain_text`. That
function calls `line.trim()` on every line, which destroys the leading
whitespace that encodes the plan tree structure. Without indentation,
depesz cannot parse the plan and returns HTTP 200 (the empty submission
form) instead of 302 + Location.

`execute_query` had already stored the correctly-indented plan text in
`last_explain_text` (from the raw PostgreSQL row values). The overwrite in
`execute_query_interactive` was redundant and incorrect.

**Fix:** remove the two `last_explain_text =` assignments from
`execute_query_interactive`. The value stored by `execute_query` is
authoritative and preserves indentation.

## Demo proof

Tested against `demo_saas` with `explain analyze select * from orders limit 10;`:

- `\explain share depesz` ‚Üí https://explain.depesz.com/s/EcDq
- `\explain share dalibo` ‚Üí https://explain.dalibo.com/plan/e16a0763a91h8g7d

Both URLs open valid visualiser pages with the uploaded plan.

## Checklist

- [x] `cargo fmt --check` ‚Äî clean
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` ‚Äî clean
- [x] `cargo test` ‚Äî 1650 passed, 0 failed
- [x] Live demo with real URLs confirmed above

mergedPRs.title: fix(explain): fix non-analyze rendering and Limit false positives
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

Fixes #666. Three interconnected bugs in EXPLAIN rendering:

- **Empty summary for plain EXPLAIN**: `explain select pg_sleep(1);` showed a blank summary header with no metrics. Now shows `Estimated cost: X..Y ‚îÇ Estimated rows: N` for any plain EXPLAIN.
- **Custom tree replaced raw plan**: The enhanced renderer was replacing PostgreSQL's full plan output with a custom tree that omitted costs, filters, and width. Now the summary header appears on top and the **full raw plan is preserved**, with ANSI color overlaid (node lines colored, detail lines dimmed, timing lines bold-white).
- **Indentation lost in strip**: `strip_psql_table_format` was calling `.trim()` on every line, destroying the indentation that the parser uses to reconstruct parent-child relationships. Root and child nodes were becoming siblings, causing Seq Scans to be treated as root nodes (wrong estimated_rows) and breaking the Limit false-positive fix.
- **Limit false-positive WARN**: `explain analyze select * from t limit 10;` would incorrectly warn "Row estimate overestimated by Nx" on the Seq Scan child. The child's estimated_rows reflects the full table, not a planning error ‚Äî the Limit stops execution early. Now suppressed for nodes that are constrained by a Limit ancestor with matching actual_rows.

mergedPRs.title: fix(ai): reset TxState to Idle after /ask read-only tx
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Bug

After `/ask` executes a query wrapped in `start transaction read only; ...; commit;`, `update_from_sql()` only parses the leading `start transaction` keywords and sets `TxState::InTransaction`. The trailing `commit;` is never processed, leaving the prompt stuck at `=*#` (in-transaction) even though the DB session is idle.

Root cause: `update_from_sql()` only looks at the first 3 words of the SQL string and cannot parse a multi-statement batch.

## Fix

In `src/repl/ai_commands.rs`, after `execute_query_interactive` returns for the wrapped `/ask` read-only query:

- **Both `AskChoice::Yes` and `AskChoice::Edit` paths**: unconditionally set `*tx = TxState::Idle` after the call returns (success or error/rollback ‚Äî both leave the session idle)
- Also call the new `StatusLine::set_tx_state(TxState::Idle)` so the status bar is corrected immediately (not just the prompt)
- Added `StatusLine::set_tx_state()` helper in `src/statusline.rs`

## Demo proof

Before fix: prompt showed `=*#` and statusline showed `tx:in-tx` after `/ask`.

After fix (captured from tmux):

To find out how many customers are on the free plan, you can run the following SQL query:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS free_plan_customers
FROM customers
WHERE plan = 'free';

---------------------
                1662
(1 row)

demo_saas=#
 /tmp:5432/demo_saas ‚îÇ SQL ‚îÇ tx:idle ‚îÇ last: 2ms ‚îÇ ai: 384/0tok

Prompt shows `=#` (not `=*#`) and statusline shows `tx:idle` after both `/ask` commands.

## Test plan

mergedPRs.title: fix(safety): handle WHERE on its own line in DELETE/UPDATE
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- `check_segment()` used `upper.contains(" WHERE ")` which requires a literal space on both sides of WHERE, causing a false-positive destructive-operation warning when WHERE appears on its own line (common in LLM-generated SQL).
- Replaced both the `DELETE` and `UPDATE` branches with `tokens.contains(&"WHERE")`, reusing the `split_whitespace` token vec already computed in the function ‚Äî no extra allocation or regex.
- Added 4 new unit tests: multiline DELETE with unindented WHERE, multiline DELETE with indented WHERE, multiline UPDATE with unindented WHERE, multiline UPDATE with indented WHERE.

mergedPRs.title: fix(pager): clear screen after leaving alternate buffer
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Ghost characters from prior pager content bled into the visible area after pressing `q` to exit the built-in TUI pager
- Root cause: `TerminalGuard::drop` called `LeaveAlternateScreen` but did not clear the main screen buffer before returning to the REPL
- Fix: emit cursor-home (`\x1b[H`) + erase-entire-display (`\x1b[2J`) immediately after `LeaveAlternateScreen` in `TerminalGuard::drop`, giving the REPL a clean slate every time the pager exits

## Demo proof

Tested in a 130x40 tmux window with `PGDATABASE=demo_saas`:

**During pager** (`\?` triggered pager):
```
rpg 0.7.0 (4b8ccb5, built 2026-03-20)

Backslash commands:
  \q              quit rpg
  ...
 Lines 1-39 of 93 (0%) ‚Äî q:quit ‚Üë‚Üì:scroll PgUp/PgDn:page /:search f:freeze
```

**After pressing `q`** ‚Äî clean screen, no ghost characters:
```
demo_saas=#     ‚Üê prompt at top, no bleed-through content below

 /tmp:5432/demo_saas ‚îÇ SQL ‚îÇ tx:idle   ‚Üê status bar at bottom
```

All 1668 unit tests pass.

## Test plan

- [ ] Run a query to confirm the REPL works normally after pager exit

mergedPRs.title: fix(ci): pass CODECOV_TOKEN to reusable checks workflow
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- **Root cause:** GitHub Actions does not automatically pass secrets to reusable workflows called via `workflow_call`. The `Coverage` job in `checks.yml` was receiving an empty token, and Codecov rejected the upload with `"Token required because branch is protected"`.
- Add `secrets: inherit` in `ci.yml` when invoking `checks.yml`, so `CODECOV_TOKEN` is forwarded.
- Declare `CODECOV_TOKEN` in `checks.yml`'s `workflow_call.secrets` block (required to accept inherited secrets).
- Switch `fail_ci_if_error: true` and add `verbose: true` so future upload failures surface as CI failures rather than being silently swallowed.

## Diagnosis evidence (from CI logs)

```
-> Token of length 0 detected
error - Upload failed: {"message":"Token required because branch is protected"}
```

## Test plan

- [ ] CI runs green on this PR
- [ ] After merge, the next push to `main` should show coverage data in Codecov and the badge should change from "unknown" to a percentage

mergedPRs.title: fix(ci): fix codecov badge
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- The codecov badge was showing "unknown" because the `CODECOV_TOKEN` secret was missing from the `codecov/codecov-action` upload step
- Without the token, Codecov rejects uploads silently (the step has `fail_ci_if_error: false`, so CI stayed green)
- Added `token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}` to authenticate the upload

## Root cause

The coverage job in `.github/workflows/checks.yml` generates `lcov.info` correctly via `cargo-llvm-cov`, but the subsequent upload step lacked authentication. Codecov v5+ requires a token for private repos and strongly recommends it for public repos to associate uploads correctly.

mergedPRs.title: feat(repl): add Lua-based custom meta commands
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Lua 5.4 runtime (via `mlua` crate, feature-gated as `--features lua`) so users can write custom backslash commands in `~/.config/rpg/commands/*.lua`
- New `\commands` meta-command lists all loaded custom Lua commands with descriptions
- Unknown backslash commands are matched against the registry before the "invalid command" error fires, so `\my_cmd arg1 arg2` Just Works once a script is installed
- Lua API: `rpg.register_command`, `rpg.query` (read-only tx), `rpg.print`, `rpg.pager`, `rpg.version`, `rpg.dbname`
- Two example scripts in `examples/commands/`: `bloat_check.lua` and `db_info.lua`
- When the `lua` feature is not compiled in, the registry is always empty and execution returns a graceful error message

## Architecture

- `src/lua_commands.rs` ‚Äî `LuaRegistry` struct, command discovery, `rpg` module installation, handler invocation
- `src/metacmd.rs` ‚Äî added `MetaCmd::ListCustomCommands` variant + `\commands` parser
- `src/repl/mod.rs` ‚Äî `lua_registry` field on `ReplSettings`, loaded at session start; dispatch in `dispatch_meta` for both `ListCustomCommands` and fallback in the `Unknown` branch
- `rpg.query()` uses a thread-local client pointer + `block_in_place` / `Handle::block_on` for safe sync execution inside an async context

## Test plan

- [ ] `cargo test` ‚Äî all 1643 tests pass

mergedPRs.title: fix(repl): auto-rollback stale transactions after /ask errors
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Fixes #638

## Summary

- Add `internal_tx: bool` flag to `ReplSettings` to track whether an open transaction was started by the internal `/ask` machinery (vs. an explicit user `BEGIN`). Only internal transactions are eligible for auto-rollback, preventing silent rollback of user transactions.
- Add `auto_rollback_stale_tx` async helper that checks `internal_tx && (InTransaction | Failed)`, issues `ROLLBACK`, resets `TxState::Idle`, clears the flag, and prints `-- auto-rolled back stale transaction`.
- Call `auto_rollback_stale_tx` at the top of `handle_line` so every incoming REPL command cleans up stale state before executing.
- In `handle_ai_ask`: set `internal_tx = true` before wrapping and executing the read-only SQL; after the error-path `client.simple_query("rollback")`, explicitly reset `*tx = TxState::Idle`; clear `internal_tx = false` on both success and failure paths. Same fix applied to the `AskChoice::Edit` path.
- Add 4 unit tests for detection logic: stale `InTransaction`, stale `Failed`, no rollback for user `BEGIN`, no rollback when `Idle`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `cargo test` ‚Äî all 1532 tests pass, 4 new unit tests added
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` ‚Äî clean
- [ ] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` ‚Äî clean
- [ ] Manual: trigger `/ask` query, hit Ctrl-C mid-query ‚Äî next command shows `-- auto-rolled back stale transaction` and prompt returns to `mydb=>`
- [ ] Manual: explicit `BEGIN; select 1;` without `COMMIT` ‚Äî next command does NOT auto-rollback (user transaction preserved)

mergedPRs.title: fix(pager): guard against non-TTY stdin before starting pager
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Fixes "Failed to initialize input reader" when `\?` or other backslash commands trigger the built-in pager in non-interactive/piped mode
- Root cause: on macOS, crossterm's `mio`/`kqueue` event reader fails to register a newly-opened `/dev/tty` fd (`EVFILT_READ` returns `EINVAL`). This happens when stdin is a pipe and `tty_fd()` opens `/dev/tty` as a new fd instead of reusing the inherited stdin fd 0. The global `InternalEventReader` ends up with `source=None`, causing every subsequent `event::poll()` to return "Failed to initialize input reader".
- Fix: add an early stdin TTY check in `run_pager()` ‚Äî return `Err(Unsupported)` immediately when stdin is not a terminal. All three call sites (`repl/execute.rs`, `dba.rs`, `describe.rs`) suppress the error print for `Unsupported` errors (expected in piped/non-interactive mode) and fall back to plain `print!`.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build` succeeds
- [x] `cargo test` ‚Äî 1528 tests pass
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` passes
- [x] Verified no "pager error" in piped mode: `printf "\\\\?\n\\\\q\n" | rpg -d postgres 2>&1 | grep "pager error"` ‚Äî empty
- [x] Verified help text still prints in piped mode: `printf "\\\\?\n\\\\q\n" | rpg -d postgres 2>&1 | grep "Backslash commands"` ‚Äî found

openPRs.title: fix(connection): surface real IO error instead of 'db error' on connect failure
openPRs.author: NikolayS
openPRs.body:
Fixes #707

## Problem

Users got `connection failed: db error` with no useful information. Reported by @mlusetti on X.

## Root cause

`tokio_postgres::Error::to_string()` returns the opaque string `"db error"` / `"error connecting to server"` when the error is not a DB-level error. The actual IO error (TCP refused, DNS failure, etc.) is buried in the `source()` chain.

## Fix

Walk the `std::error::Error::source()` chain in `map_connect_error()` to find the deepest meaningful message.

## Before / After

```
# Wrong port:
Before: connection to server at "localhost", port 9999 failed: db error
After:  connection to server at "localhost", port 9999 failed: Connection refused (os error 111)

# Wrong hostname:
Before: connection to server at "bad.host", port 5432 failed: db error
After:  connection to server at "bad.host", port 5432 failed: failed to lookup address information: Name or service not known
```

openPRs.title: feat(wasi): implement WSS client backend
openPRs.author: NikolayS
openPRs.body:
## Summary

- Add `src/wasi_client.rs`: sync WebSocket PG client for the WASI build target
- Use `tungstenite` (no-default-features + handshake) for WebSocket over TCP
- Implement minimal PG wire protocol: StartupMessage, Query, RowDescription, DataRow, CommandComplete, ErrorResponse, Terminate
- Config from `PG_WSS_URL` / `PGUSER` / `PGPASSWORD` / `PGDATABASE` env vars plus psql-compatible `-U`/`-d`/`-c` flags
- Gate all native modules with `#[cfg(not(feature = "wasi"))]` ‚Äî zero tokio/TUI/rustls/SSH deps in WASI build
- Move native-only deps to `[target.'cfg(not(target_os = "wasi"))'.dependencies]` so they are excluded from WASI compilation

Closes #699

## How to test

Start the WSS proxy:
```bash
node /tmp/wss-pg-proxy.js 8080 localhost 5432 &
```

Build and run (requires `wasm32-wasip2` for outbound TCP; `wasm32-wasip1` does not support `sock_connect` in wasmtime 43):
```bash
cargo build --target wasm32-wasip2 --features wasi --no-default-features

wasmtime -S tcp -S allow-ip-name-lookup -S inherit-network \
  --env PG_WSS_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:8080 \
  --env PGUSER=postgres \
  target/wasm32-wasip2/debug/rpg.wasm \
  -- -c "SELECT version()"
```

Expected output:
```
version
PostgreSQL 17.7 ...
(1 row)
```

Verify native build is unaffected:
```bash
cargo build
cargo clippy -- -D warnings
```

## Notes

- `wasm32-wasip2` used instead of `wasm32-wasip1` because WASI Preview 1 does not include `sock_connect` for outbound TCP; Preview 2 has full socket support via `wasi:sockets`
- Trust auth and cleartext-password auth supported; SCRAM is a follow-up
- The `lua_commands.rs` pre-existing `clippy::unused_self` lint is suppressed with `#[allow]`

- Long queries truncated with ‚Ä¶ to terminal width

## Backwards compatibility

- `\s pattern` ‚Äî still works (non-interactive filter ‚Üí pager)
- `\s filename` ‚Äî save to file, unchanged

## Implementation

New file `src/history_picker.rs` (276 lines): ratatui-based modal, reads from `read_history_entries()`, returns `Option<String>`.

`dispatch_history()` in `src/repl/mod.rs`: when `arg.is_none()`, calls picker instead of pager dump. Uses `readline_with_initial` to pre-fill the selected entry.

Gated with `#[cfg(not(feature = "wasi"))]`.

## Out of scope (follow-up issues)

- Ctrl-R binding (#693)
- AI-powered recall (`/recall`)
- Frecency sorting
- Per-database history

openPRs.title: feat(wasi): WASI target for browser embedding via WSS proxy
openPRs.author: NikolayS
openPRs.body:
Closes #699

## What

Scaffold for compiling rpg to `wasm32-wasip1` (WASI), enabling it to run in a browser or WASI runtime and connect to Postgres via the existing WSS proxy (febe proto).

## Changes so far

- `rust-toolchain.toml`: adds `wasm32-wasip1` target
- `Cargo.toml`: adds `wasi` feature flag (gates WSS proxy backend vs native TCP)
- `.github/workflows/checks.yml`: adds `WASI Build` CI job

## What's needed next (for Claude Code to implement)

1. Feature-gate native TCP layer: wrap `tokio-postgres` connection code in `#[cfg(not(feature = "wasi"))]`
2. Add WASI socket backend stub under `#[cfg(feature = "wasi")]` ‚Äî connects via WSS proxy instead of raw TCP
3. Fix `tokio` rt: swap `rt-multi-thread` ‚Üí `rt` for WASI target (via `[target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]`)
4. Fix `ring`/`rustls` for WASI target
5. Get clean `cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --features wasi`
6. Add `crossterm` WASM backend feature flag for TUI rendering

## WSS proxy

WSS proxy (febe proto) already exists ‚Äî handles WebSocket‚ÜîPostgres protocol bridging. rpg WASI build connects to it instead of raw TCP.

mergedPRs.title: fix: revert ignores -- sqlever:no-transaction directive
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Bug found by QA: singleTransaction hardcoded to true in revert. Now reads the directive from revert scripts like deploy does.

## Summary
- In `src/commands/revert.ts`, `singleTransaction` was hardcoded to `true` when calling `psqlRunner.run`, causing `DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY` (and other non-transactional commands) in revert scripts to fail
- Now reads revert script content before execution and checks for `-- sqlever:no-transaction` using the same `isNonTransactional()` function from `deploy.ts`
- Added 3 tests verifying the directive is respected in revert context and that the hardcoded value is gone

## Test plan
- [x] Existing revert tests pass (42/42)
- [x] New test: revert script with `-- sqlever:no-transaction` sets `singleTransaction: false`
- [x] New test: normal revert script keeps `singleTransaction: true`
- [x] New test: source code no longer contains hardcoded `singleTransaction: true`

mergedPRs.title: test: deploy/revert robustness tests (issue #125)
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary
- Add `tests/unit/deploy-revert-robustness.test.ts` with 40 tests covering 7 categories of deploy/revert robustness:
  1. **Transaction semantics (5)**: `--mode change` per-change txn, `--mode all`/`--mode tag` rejected as not-yet-implemented, unknown mode error, non-transactional change skips `--single-transaction`
  2. **Non-transactional handling (7)**: CIC without BEGIN, `-- sqlever:no-transaction` marker detection, tracking record lifecycle, failed CIC leaves no tracking record, next deploy re-attempts failed change
  3. **Advisory lock (7)**: `pg_try_advisory_lock` returns false -> exit 4, lock key stability (same project = same key), different projects = different keys, lock released on success/failure/validation-failure, lock auto-released on disconnect
  4. **Failure recovery (6)**: SQL error -> tracking unchanged, mid-batch failure (3 changes, 2nd fails), revert failure records fail event, DB unreachable exit code 10, partial state correctness, fail event recorded
  5. **Lock timeout guard (5)**: auto-prepend SET lock_timeout, skip when script has own SET, configurable timeout value, retry with backoff, isLockTimeoutError detection
  6. **SIGINT/SIGTERM (5)**: shutting-down flag, cleanup callbacks run, exit 130 for SIGINT, exit 143 for SIGTERM, second signal force-exits
  7. **Revert robustness (5)**: correct reverse order, `--to` validation (missing value errors), `-y` required for non-TTY, returns exit code (no process.exit), fail event on revert error

Closes #125

mergedPRs.title: F3: Batch monitoring ‚Äî replication lag + VACUUM pressure
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Add `src/batch/progress.ts` implementing SPEC Section 5.5 batch job progress monitoring
- **Row counting**: `calculateProgress()` computes rows done/total, percentage, ETA from observed throughput
- **Replication lag**: queries `pg_stat_replication.replay_lag`, pauses batch when lag exceeds configurable threshold (default 10s)
- **VACUUM pressure**: queries `pg_stat_user_tables.n_dead_tup`, computes dead tuple ratio `n_dead_tup/(n_live_tup+n_dead_tup)`, pauses when >10% (configurable via `sqlever.toml` `[batch] max_dead_tuple_ratio`)
- **Heartbeat staleness**: detects dead workers when heartbeat exceeds threshold (default 5min), handles OOM kill / network partition
- **pg_stat_activity**: shows current batch queries with duration, wait events, and state
- Combined `shouldPause()` method checks replication lag then VACUUM pressure
- Pure functions (`calculateProgress`, `parseIntervalToSeconds`, `computeDeadTupleRatio`, `checkHeartbeatStaleness`) separated from DB-dependent `ProgressMonitor` class for testability

mergedPRs.title: F1: PGQ-style 3-partition batch queue with SKIP LOCKED
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

Implements #102 ‚Äî PGQ-style 3-partition rotating queue for batched background DML (SPEC DD9 + Section 5.5).

- **3-partition rotation** (DD9): Three partitions rotate (active/processing/drain). Completed batches cleaned via `TRUNCATE` ‚Äî instant, zero bloat, no VACUUM pressure. Solves the dead-tuple accumulation problem that `DELETE` would cause.
- **SKIP LOCKED dequeue** (DD9): `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` lets multiple batch workers dequeue concurrently without blocking each other. Complementary to partition rotation (rotation = cleanup, SKIP LOCKED = concurrency).
- **Job lifecycle**: `pending -> running -> done | failed -> dead` with retry support. Dead jobs can be manually retried after operator fixes the underlying issue.
- **Heartbeat staleness detection**: `heartbeat_at` column with configurable threshold (default 5 min). Detects silently dead workers (OOM kill, network partition) and marks their jobs as failed.
- **DDL generation**: `sqlever.batch_jobs` partitioned table with 3 child partitions, partial indexes for pending/running status, and `batch_queue_meta` for partition role tracking.

## Files

- `src/batch/queue.ts` ‚Äî `BatchQueue` class, DDL generation, job lifecycle, partition rotation
- `tests/unit/batch-queue.test.ts` ‚Äî 63 tests covering all acceptance criteria

## Test plan

- [x] Partition rotation: TRUNCATE drain partition, rotate roles correctly through full cycle (0->2->1->0)
- [x] SKIP LOCKED dequeue: query uses `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, returns null when empty, sets heartbeat
- [x] Job lifecycle: all valid transitions (pending->running, running->done, running->failed, failed->running, failed->dead, dead->running), invalid transitions rejected
- [x] Heartbeat staleness: detects stale jobs, marks as failed/dead based on retry count, custom threshold
- [x] DDL: 3 partitions, partial indexes, CHECK constraints, metadata table
- [x] 63 tests total (requirement: >= 20)

Closes #102

## Summary
- Add `--no-snapshot` flag to the deploy command that skips git-correlated include resolution and uses HEAD/current files for `\i`/`\ir` directives (Sqitch-compatible behavior)

mergedPRs.title: C1: snapshot includes ‚Äî git-correlated \i/\ir resolution
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Implement `src/includes/snapshot.ts` for resolving psql `\i` and `\ir` include directives from the git commit where the migration was added to the plan (SPEC Section 5.2)
- Ensures fresh-database deploys use historically-correct file versions by retrieving includes via `git show <commit>:<path>` instead of HEAD
- Fallback chain: specific commit -> HEAD -> working tree (for non-git environments)
- 52 tests using real temporary git repositories covering parsing, resolution, nesting, circular detection, and edge cases

## Details

**Core API:**
- `parseIncludeDirective()` / `findIncludes()` ‚Äî parse `\i`, `\ir`, `\include`, `\include_relative` directives, skipping SQL comments
- `resolveIncludePath()` ‚Äî `\i` relative to repo root, `\ir` relative to script directory
- `getFileAtCommit()` ‚Äî retrieve file content via `git show <commit>:<path>`
- `findCommitByTimestamp()` ‚Äî map `planned_at` timestamp to nearest git commit
- `resolveIncludes()` ‚Äî recursive resolution with circular include detection and max depth enforcement
- `resolveDeployIncludes()` ‚Äî high-level API for deploy integration

**Key behaviors:**
- Nested includes: `\i a.sql` where `a.sql` also has `\i b.sql` resolves correctly
- Diamond dependencies (A->B, A->C, B->D, C->D) resolve without false circular detection
- Circular includes detected with clear error messages
- Diagnostic comments (`-- [snapshot] begin/end include`) added around inlined content
- Graceful fallback when git is unavailable or file has no history

mergedPRs.title: S5-3: Analysis rules SA011-SA021 ‚Äî DML, DDL, and locking safety rules
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

Implements the remaining 11 static analysis rules from SPEC section 5.1, completing the full SA001-SA021 rule set:

- **SA011** (connected, warn): UPDATE/DELETE on large table ‚Äî flags DML on tables with high estimated row counts when a DB connection is available
- **SA012** (static, info): ALTER SEQUENCE RESTART ‚Äî informational flag for sequence restarts that may break ID assumptions
- **SA013** (static, warn): Missing SET lock_timeout before risky DDL ‚Äî detects ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX, DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, CLUSTER, VACUUM FULL, and REINDEX without a preceding lock_timeout guard
- **SA014** (static, warn): VACUUM FULL or CLUSTER ‚Äî flags operations that take AccessExclusiveLock and rewrite the table
- **SA015** (static, warn): ALTER TABLE RENAME (table or column) ‚Äî flags renames that break running application code
- **SA016** (static, error): ADD CONSTRAINT CHECK without NOT VALID ‚Äî blocks deploy for CHECK constraints that scan under heavy lock
- **SA017** (hybrid, warn): SET NOT NULL on existing column ‚Äî flags full table scan risk, with connected suppression stub
- **SA018** (hybrid, warn): ADD PRIMARY KEY without USING INDEX ‚Äî flags extended AccessExclusiveLock during implicit index creation
- **SA019** (static, warn): REINDEX without CONCURRENTLY ‚Äî flags AccessExclusiveLock on reindex
- **SA020** (static, error): CONCURRENTLY operations in transactional context ‚Äî blocks deploy for operations that cannot run in transactions
- **SA021** (static, warn): Explicit LOCK TABLE ‚Äî flags dangerous explicit locking in migrations

## Details

- All rules follow the exact same patterns as SA001-SA010: standalone exported function, AnalysisContext input, Finding[] output
- Registry updated from 11 to 22 rules in `src/analysis/rules/index.ts`
- 174 new tests in `tests/unit/analysis-rules-11-21.test.ts`
- 55 no-trigger fixture files (5 per rule) + 28 trigger fixtures across `tests/fixtures/analysis/SA0{11-21}/`
- Updated existing test to expect 22 rules in registry
- All 348 tests pass across both test files

- [x] `bun test tests/unit/analysis-rules-11-21.test.ts` ‚Äî 174 pass, 0 fail
- [x] `bun test tests/unit/analysis-rules.test.ts` ‚Äî 174 pass, 0 fail (existing tests still pass)

mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Implement 11 analysis rules (SA001-SA010) per SPEC Section 5.1, one file per rule in `src/analysis/rules/`
- Add shared types (`Rule`, `Finding`, `AnalysisContext`, `offsetToLocation`) in `src/analysis/types.ts`

- 174 tests covering trigger cases, no-trigger cases, edge cases, PL/pgSQL body exclusion, version-aware behavior, and message/location quality

### Rules implemented

| Rule | Severity | Type | Trigger |
|------|----------|------|---------|
| SA001 | error | static | ADD COLUMN NOT NULL without DEFAULT |
| SA002 | error | static | ADD COLUMN DEFAULT volatile (any PG) |
| SA002b | warn | static | ADD COLUMN DEFAULT non-volatile (PG < 11) |
| SA003 | error | static | ALTER COLUMN TYPE (unsafe cast / USING) |
| SA004 | warn | static | CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY |
| SA005 | warn | static | DROP INDEX without CONCURRENTLY |
| SA006 | warn | static | DROP COLUMN |
| SA007 | error | static | DROP TABLE (non-revert context) |
| SA008 | warn | static | TRUNCATE |
| SA009 | warn | hybrid | ADD FOREIGN KEY without NOT VALID |
| SA010 | warn | static | UPDATE/DELETE without WHERE |

### Design notes

- Each rule is a standalone function that takes a parsed AST and returns `Finding[]`, ready for integration with the analysis engine (issue #50)
- Uses `libpg-query` WASM module for SQL parsing in tests
- SA002 detects volatile functions (`random()`, `gen_random_uuid()`, `clock_timestamp()`, `txid_current()`, etc.) recursively through type casts and expressions
- SA002b is version-aware: only fires when `pgVersion < 11`
- SA003 fires on all type changes in static mode (conservative); USING clause always triggers
- SA007 respects `isRevertContext` flag for sqitch revert script exemption
- SA008/SA010 implement PL/pgSQL body exclusion per spec
- SA009 is typed as hybrid with static NOT VALID check implemented; connected index check is stubbed for DB integration

mergedPRs.title: fix: revert --to validation + advisory lock leak
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary
- **Bug 1 (`--to` without value):** `revert --to` with no argument silently fell through to reverting ALL deployed changes. Now validates that `--to` has a non-empty, non-flag value and throws a clear error (`Missing value for --to. Usage: revert --to <change>`).
- **Bug 2 (advisory lock leak):** All `process.exit()` calls inside `runRevert`'s try block bypassed the finally block, leaking the PostgreSQL advisory lock. Replaced every `process.exit()` in `runRevert` with `return <exitCode>` (changed return type from `Promise<void>` to `Promise<number>`). The caller in `cli.ts` now calls `process.exit()` after `runRevert` resolves, so the finally block always runs and releases the lock.
- Added 5 new tests covering both bugs: `--to` with missing value, `--to` followed by a flag, source-level assertion that `process.exit` is absent from `revert.ts`, and runtime verification that `runRevert` returns a numeric exit code.

mergedPRs.title: S4-7: sqlever rework ‚Äî rework an existing change
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary
- Implements `sqlever rework <change> [-n note]` command with full Sqitch rework semantics (closes #49)
- Verifies the change exists in the plan and has a tag after its last occurrence, copies current deploy/revert/verify scripts to `<change>@<tag>.sql` backups, creates fresh template scripts for the new version, and appends a reworked entry to the plan with `[change@tag]` dependency
- Wires the command into the CLI dispatcher alongside `init` and `add`
- 28 tests covering: argument parsing (3), plan analysis/findReworkContext (7), runRework integration (14), and CLI subprocess (4)

mergedPRs.title: S4-4: sqlever tag -- create tag at current deployment state
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary
- Implements the `tag` command (`src/commands/tag.ts`) that creates a tag at the current plan state, attached to the last change
- Computes Sqitch-compatible tag IDs via `computeTagId` (SHA-1 envelope format)
- Appends tag entries to `sqitch.plan` using `appendTag` from the plan writer
- Supports `-n`/`--note` flag and automatic `@` prefix stripping
- Wires tag into CLI dispatch in `src/cli.ts`
- 26 tests covering: arg parsing, tag name validation, ID computation, duplicate detection, empty-plan errors, multi-change plans, URI handling, roundtrip parseability, and CLI subprocess integration

Closes #46

- [x] Tag ID computed correctly matches `computeTagId` output

mergedPRs.title: S3-3: Revert command
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary
- Implement `sqlever revert [target] [--to change] [-y]` per SPEC R1 and issue #34
- Full revert flow: advisory lock, deployed change lookup, reverse-order execution via PsqlRunner, tracking table updates, graceful failure handling with `fail` event recording
- Non-TTY stdin detection requires `-y` or errors out (CI safety)
- Add `Registry.recordFailEvent()` to record failures without deleting changes from tracking tables

## Changes
- **`src/commands/revert.ts`** (new) ‚Äî Core revert command: argument parsing, target URI resolution, confirmation prompt, change computation, script execution loop, fail-event recording, advisory lock lifecycle
- **`src/db/registry.ts`** ‚Äî Add `recordFailEvent()` method for recording `fail` events without deleting change/dependency rows
- **`src/cli.ts`** ‚Äî Wire `revert` command into CLI router
- **`tests/unit/revert.test.ts`** (new) ‚Äî 33 unit tests covering: argument parsing (8), revert ordering (8), input building (3), confirmation prompt (2), target URI resolution (4), Registry.recordFailEvent (2), integration scenarios (3), CLI routing (3)
- **`tests/unit/cli.test.ts`** ‚Äî Remove `revert` from stub command list

## Test plan
- [x] 33 new unit tests pass (`bun test tests/unit/revert.test.ts`)
- [x] Full suite: 733 tests pass, 0 failures (`bun test`)
- [x] No new TypeScript errors (`bun x tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] Integration: deploy 3+ changes, `revert --to <mid>` reverts only later ones
- [ ] Integration: `revert -y` skips prompt
- [ ] Integration: non-revertable migration (exception in revert script) records `fail` event, exits 1

Closes #34
